Declares a function to get the button ID for app launch under specific conditions.

# Purpose
This header file declares a function [`app_launch_button`](<#app_launch_button>) that returns a `ButtonId`. The function is intended to retrieve the button identifier used to launch an application. It is only valid when the launch reason is either `APP_LAUNCH_USER` or `APP_LAUNCH_QUICK_LAUNCH`. The file includes a dependency on `drivers/button_id.h`, which likely defines the `ButtonId` type. The use of `#pragma once` ensures the file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `drivers/button_id.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### app\_launch\_button<!-- {{#callable_declaration:app_launch_button}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/applib/app_launch_button.h#L24>)

Get the button ID used to launch the app.
- **Description**: Use this function to obtain the button identifier that initiated the app launch. It is valid only when the launch reason is either `APP_LAUNCH_USER` or `APP_LAUNCH_QUICK_LAUNCH`. Ensure that the launch reason is checked before calling this function to avoid invalid results.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns a `ButtonId` that represents the button used to launch the app.
- **See Also**: [`app_launch_button`](<app_launch_button.c.md#app_launch_button>)  (Implementation)
